title: fix: tests webserver syntaxwarning
body:
> Changed in Python version 3.12: Unrecognized escape sequences produce a SyntaxWarning. In a future Python version they will be eventually a SyntaxError.
[2.4.1.1. Escape sequences](https://docs.python.org/3/reference/lexical_analysis.html#index-23)

Change to raw string literal to fix:
```
/webserver.py:17: SyntaxWarning: invalid escape sequence '\d'
  elif re.match("/status/(\d+)", self.path):
/webserver.py:18: SyntaxWarning: invalid escape sequence '\d'
  result = re.match("/status/(\d+)", self.path)
```

step_plan.summary: Update the test webserver’s `/status/<code>` route matching to use a raw-string regex literal so Python 3.12+ no longer emits `SyntaxWarning: invalid escape sequence '\d'`, while keeping the route behavior unchanged.

steps.operation: Fix invalid escape sequence in /status/<code> route regex by converting the pattern to a raw string literal (and reuse the match object to avoid duplicate re.match calls).
steps.reason: Python 3.12+ emits a SyntaxWarning for unrecognized escape sequences like '\d' inside non-raw string literals; using a raw string (e.g., r"/status/(\d+)") preserves the intended regex semantics and prevents the warning (and future SyntaxError). Reusing the match object keeps behavior identical while removing redundant work.
steps.side_effects: No intended behavior change for routing; `/status/<digits>` should continue to return the parsed status code. Affects only the test webserver; may require adjusting/confirming any tests that depend on exact matching semantics, but `re.match` with the same pattern should behave the same. Running tests under Python 3.12 should no longer show the SyntaxWarning from this file.
